sections.paragraph:
φιλόφρων, ον, gen. ονος (s. prec. entry; Pind., X. et al.; PGrenf I 30, 5 [II b.c.]) well-disposed, friendly, kind 1 Pt 3:8 v.l.—DELG s.v. φρήν II.

gen. gen. = genitive

prec. prec. = preceding

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1060
